context_prose: Scoped slice of [c3-engram-cli-binary.md](c3-engram-cli-binary.md): the L3 edges that touch E23. The DI back-edge convention (see legend) applies — E23 → E21 represents the category of `FileReader.Read` calls E23 makes through the dependency wired by E21 through E22 (recall). The R-edge label cites the P-list each edge backs.
legend_items: Solid grey: L3 elements carried over.
**Solid arrow (R[n])** = direct call. Standard C4 reading: "source initiates the interaction with target."

**Dotted arrow (D[n])** = DI back-edge. Project-specific convention representing "source initiates a category of calls whose concrete targets are determined by the dotted-arrow target (the wirer)." One D-id per (consumer, wirer) pair regardless of how many concrete deps; the per-dep decomposition lives in the Dependency Manifest below.
**R8:** the existing L3 element-to-element call from E22 (recall) into E23.
**D6:** the DI back-edge from E23 to E21 (cli), mirrored from L3.
name: context

properties.name: Delta from offset 0
properties.notes: Splits on `\n`; empty trailing line dropped.
properties.statement: For all transcript paths P with file content C of length N, calling `DeltaReader.Read(P, 0)` returns every non-empty line of C as the delta and N as the new offset.

properties.name: Mid-file offset delta
properties.notes: Stable byte-offset semantics enable incremental reading.
properties.statement: For all transcript paths P with content C and any offset O where 0 ≤ O ≤ len(C), `DeltaReader.Read(P, O)` returns only the lines in C[O:] and len(C) as the new offset.

properties.name: Rotation reset
properties.notes: Handles log rotation / truncation gracefully.
properties.statement: For all transcript paths P with content of length N and any offset O > N, `DeltaReader.Read(P, O)` resets to offset 0 and returns the full file as the delta.

properties.name: Empty file delta
properties.notes: Resets offset because there is nothing to read past.
properties.statement: For all transcript paths P pointing to a zero-length file and any offset O, `DeltaReader.Read(P, O)` returns a nil/empty delta and offset 0.

properties.name: FileReader error propagated
properties.notes: I/O errors surface to caller; not silently swallowed.
properties.statement: For all errors E returned by the injected `FileReader.Read`, `DeltaReader.Read` returns (nil, 0, E') where E' wraps or matches E.

properties.name: Strip total
properties.notes: Function has no error return; lack of panic asserted only indirectly by other tests. No targeted fuzz/property test for malformed bytes.
properties.statement: For all input lines L (any string content, malformed JSON, binary garbage included), `Strip(L)` returns successfully without panic and without error.

properties.name: Filter to user/assistant
properties.notes: Role-only legacy format also accepted (P8).
properties.statement: For all input lines L, `Strip(L)` emits output only for lines whose JSONL `type` or message `role` equals `"user"` or `"assistant"`; all other entry types (progress, system, file-history-snapshot, etc.) are dropped.

properties.name: Legacy role fallback
properties.notes: Backwards compatibility with older transcript schemas.
properties.statement: For all input lines L lacking an outer `type` field but carrying `message.role` equal to `"user"` or `"assistant"`, `Strip(L)` still emits the corresponding `USER:` / `ASSISTANT:` line.

properties.name: Drops tool blocks (Strip)
properties.notes: Mixed assistant messages (text + tool_use) keep only the text.
properties.statement: For all input lines L, `Strip(L)` discards every `tool_use` and `tool_result` content block; only `text` blocks contribute to the output.

properties.name: Drops system-reminders
properties.notes: Mixed blocks: only the system-reminder text is dropped; other text blocks survive.
properties.statement: For all text content T whose trimmed prefix is `<system-reminder`, T is excluded from `Strip` output regardless of whether it appears as a string content or inside a content block.

properties.name: Base64 redaction
properties.notes: Acts on the raw line, so it also redacts base64 inside otherwise-stripped tool blocks.
properties.statement: For all input lines L containing a contiguous `[A-Za-z0-9+/=]` run of length ≥ 100, `Strip(L)` replaces every such run with the literal `[base64 removed]` before extraction.

properties.name: Per-line truncation
properties.notes: Constant `maxContentBlockLen = 2000`.
properties.statement: For all extracted lines E produced by `Strip`, `len(E) ≤ 2000 + len("[truncated]")`; any longer extraction is cut to the first 2000 bytes and suffixed with `[truncated]`.

properties.name: Role prefix correctness
properties.notes: Plain-string content and array-of-blocks content both accepted.
properties.statement: For all surviving messages M with normalized role R, `Strip` emits exactly one line beginning with `"USER: "` if R = user, or `"ASSISTANT: "` if R = assistant.

properties.name: Default config equals Strip
properties.notes: Zero-value `StripConfig` is the recall-pipeline default.
properties.statement: For all input lines L, `StripWithConfig(L, StripConfig{})` returns a result equal to `Strip(L)`.

properties.name: KeepToolCalls preserves tools
properties.notes: `is_error` true → `[error]`; false → `[ok]`.
properties.statement: For all input lines L containing `tool_use` and `tool_result` blocks, `StripWithConfig(L, StripConfig{KeepToolCalls: true})` emits a `TOOL_USE [Name]: <args>` line for each tool_use and a `TOOL_RESULT [ok|error]: <content>` line for each tool_result, in source order.

properties.name: ToolArgs/ResultTruncate honored
properties.notes: Only effective when `KeepToolCalls` is true and `ToolSummaryMode` is false.
properties.statement: For all positive integer caps `A = ToolArgsTruncate` and `R = ToolResultTruncate`, args longer than A bytes and result content longer than R bytes are truncated to that length and suffixed with `[truncated]`; zero means no truncation.

properties.name: ToolSummaryMode precedence
properties.notes: Documented in the StripConfig comment.
properties.statement: For all `StripConfig` C where `C.ToolSummaryMode` is true, `StripWithConfig` produces tool-summary output regardless of `C.KeepToolCalls`, `C.ToolArgsTruncate`, or `C.ToolResultTruncate`.

properties.name: Tool summary pairing
properties.notes: exit 0 when `is_error == false`, exit 1 when true.
properties.statement: For all tool_use blocks U with id I followed (any distance) by a tool_result block with `tool_use_id` = I, ToolSummaryMode emits exactly one `[tool] <Name>(<args>) → exit <0|1> | <first non-empty line>` line; orphaned tool_use (no matching result) emits nothing.

properties.name: Tool summary 120-char caps
properties.notes: Constants `toolSummaryArgsCap = 120`, `toolSummaryOutputCap = 120`.
properties.statement: For all tool summaries produced by ToolSummaryMode, the args portion inside the parentheses and the post-`|` first-line portion are each ≤ 120 + len("[truncated]") bytes.

properties.name: Tool summary args determinism
properties.notes: Sorted via `sort.Strings`. Determinism is asserted indirectly by `ContainSubstring`-style tests but no test pins the ordering across multiple keys.
properties.statement: For all `tool_use.input` JSON objects, `formatToolSummaryArgs` emits `key=value` pairs in lexicographic order of keys.

properties.name: Tool summary first-line only
properties.notes: Empty content omits the `| …` suffix entirely.
properties.statement: For all tool_result content blocks C, the post-`|` portion of the emitted summary is the first non-blank line of C (after `strings.TrimSpace`); subsequent lines never appear.

properties.name: Unknown block types ignored
properties.notes: Forward compatibility with new block types.
properties.statement: For all content blocks B whose `type` is not `text`, `tool_use`, or `tool_result` (e.g. `image`), B contributes nothing to `StripWithConfig` output in any mode.

properties.name: Malformed JSON dropped
properties.notes: Total-function behavior — malformed lines are silently discarded, not errored.
properties.statement: For all input lines L that fail `json.Unmarshal` into `jsonlLine` (or have an empty/unrecognized role), `Strip` and `StripWithConfig` emit no output for L.

properties.name: No direct I/O (DI-only)
properties.notes: Architectural invariant from project DI rule (CLAUDE.md "DI everywhere"). No automated guard; would need an import-scanner test or `forbidigo` lint rule.
properties.statement: For all package code in `internal/context`, no symbol references `os.*`, `io/ioutil`, `http.*`, `net.*`, or any other I/O primitive directly; the only file access is through the injected `FileReader` interface.
